question: Which high-risk imaging or clinical features are used to select patients with uncomplicated TBAD for early TEVAR?
source_access_type: Official English-language publisher PDF held in private local source workspace
relevant_sections: Chapter VI, 3.1 Acute and Subacute Dissection
PQ 11. When Should We Perform EVAR for Stanford Type B Dissection?
Table 39. Predictive Factors for Chronic-Phase Enlargement
Figure 62. Stage of Stanford Type B aortic dissection and indication of TEVAR
Table 41. COR and LOE for the Treatment of Uncomplicated Acute/Subacute Stanford Type B Aortic Dissection

primary_fields.pmid: Matched to registered PMID 37661428; PMID is not printed in the supplied PDF

source_identity_validation.metadata_warning: The title's 2020 designation is the guideline edition; the official English-language journal publication is from 2023. Japanese translations in this review are reviewer aids and are explicitly not original-source wording.

extracted_claims.claim_text: The Japanese guideline defines the acute phase as within 2 weeks, the subacute phase as the period through 3 months after onset, and uses an early-chronic preemptive-TEVAR window through 1 year.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: If complications similar to those in the acute phase appear in the subacute phase (within 3 months after onset) following the acute phase (within 2 weeks after onset), treatment should be performed in accordance with acute-phase strategy. ... subacute- to early-chronic-phase (≤1 year) TEVAR
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 急性期を発症後2週間以内、亜急性期を発症後3か月以内とし、早期慢性期を含む発症後1年以内を先制的TEVARの時期として扱っている。 The ellipsis joins two sentences on the same printed page; Figure 62 independently marks 2 weeks, 3 months, and 1 year.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; Japanese translation pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: For a high-risk uncomplicated case whose false lumen is expected to enlarge, the guideline states that preemptive TEVAR should be considered in the subacute-to-early-chronic phase.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Preemptive TEVAR should be considered in the subacute to early chronic phase for patients whose false lumen is expected to enlarge in the future: high-risk uncomplicated case
extracted_claims.source_location_note: COR IIa; LOE B. Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 将来、偽腔拡大が予測される患者、すなわち高リスク非合併症例では、亜急性期から早期慢性期の先制的TEVARを考慮すべきである。 This is selective, not routine treatment.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; Japanese translation pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline states that acute-phase preemptive TEVAR may be considered, with weaker recommendation strength than subacute-to-early-chronic selective TEVAR.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Preemptive TEVAR may be considered in the acute phase
extracted_claims.source_location_note: COR IIb; LOE B. Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 急性期の先制的TEVARを考慮してもよい。 The narrative on printed p. 1512 calls acute-phase timing controversial.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; Japanese translation pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: For uncomplicated acute-phase Stanford type B dissection, the guideline recommends medical treatment.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Medical treatment is recommended for acute-phase patients
extracted_claims.source_location_note: COR I; LOE C. Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 急性期患者には内科的治療が推奨される。 This establishes the initial-treatment context before selective preemptive TEVAR.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; Japanese translation pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline recommends TEVAR for acute/subacute complicated Stanford type B dissection as a separate indication from selective preemptive treatment.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: TEVAR is recommended for acute/subacute phases patients
extracted_claims.source_location_note: COR I; LOE C. Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 急性期・亜急性期の合併症例にはTEVARが推奨される。 Context-only claim to prevent conflation with high-risk uncomplicated disease.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; population wording pending specialist confirmation

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline separates emergent/urgent TEVAR for acute/subacute complications, preemptive TEVAR for high-risk uncomplicated disease through 1 year, and chronic-phase TEVAR for dissecting aneurysm.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: The indication of TEVAR for Type B dissection can be classified into three (Figure 62): (1) Emergent/urgent TEVAR for acute-/subacute-complicated cases (2) Preemptive TEVAR in the subacute to early chronic phases (≤1 year after onset) for high-risk uncomplicated cases (3) TEVAR in chronic-phase for dissecting aneurysm cases
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: B型大動脈解離のTEVAR適応を、(1)急性期・亜急性期の合併症例に対する緊急・準緊急TEVAR、(2)高リスク非合併症例に対する亜急性期から早期慢性期（発症後1年以内）の先制的TEVAR、(3)慢性期解離性大動脈瘤に対するTEVAR、の3つに分類している。
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; Japanese translation pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline uses 'high-risk uncomplicated dissection' for patients expected to have false-lumen enlargement and chronic-phase rupture risk despite conventional medical therapy.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Patients to be selected are those with enlargement of the false-lumen and are at risk of rupture during the chronic phase with conventional medical therapy. The condition is also termed ‘high-risk uncomplicated dissection’.
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.3 Uncomplicated Dissection, a. Definition
extracted_claims.table: Table 39 is cited for prediction parameters
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 選択対象は、従来の内科的治療では偽腔が拡大し、慢性期に破裂リスクを有する患者であり、この病態は「高リスク非合併型解離」とも呼ばれる。 This is guideline selection language, not proof that every listed predictor identifies TEVAR benefit.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; prognostic scope pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline does not recommend TEVAR for every patient with false-lumen-patent type B dissection and instead recommends selection based on expected enlargement or aneurysm formation with medical therapy alone.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Therefore, it cannot be recommended that TEVAR should be indicated for all patients with false-lumen-patent Type B dissection. Currently, it is recommended that TEVAR should be indicated for patients in whom it is expected that medical treatment alone will lead to false-lumen enlargement/aneurysm formation after a few years
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: The sentence crosses the printed-page boundary. Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 偽腔開存型B型大動脈解離の全患者をTEVAR適応とすることは推奨できず、内科的治療のみでは数年後に偽腔拡大・瘤化が予測される患者を選択すべきである。
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline states that acute-phase preemptive TEVAR timing is controversial and that the acute-phase randomized trial did not verify a 1-year prognostic advantage over medical treatment.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: On the other hand, an RCT of preemptive TEVAR in the acute phase did not verify its superiority to medical treatment for the prognosis one year after TEVAR, being contrary to the above opinion. ... This is controversial.
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.3 Uncomplicated Dissection, b. Endovascular Treatment, ii. Timing of Preemptive TEVAR
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 一方、急性期の先制的TEVARに関するRCTでは、TEVAR後1年の予後について内科的治療に対する優越性は確認されず、この点は議論がある。 A nonsignificant or unverified advantage is not treated as equivalence.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: Table 39 lists younger age, heart rate at least 60/min, Marfan syndrome or genetic aortic disease, and FDP at least 20 μg/mL as positive predictors of chronic-phase enlargement.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Age: <60 years; Heart rate: ≥60/min; Marfan syndrome and genetic aortic disease; FDP: ≥20 μg/mL
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 慢性期拡大の陽性予測因子として、60歳未満、心拍数60/分以上、Marfan症候群・遺伝性大動脈疾患、FDP 20 μg/mL以上が列挙されている。 These are predictor-table entries, not treatment-effect modifiers or outcomes.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for table transcription; validation strength pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: Table 39 lists an aortic diameter of at least 40 mm as a positive predictor of chronic-phase enlargement.
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 大動脈径40 mm以上。 Baseline prediction threshold; not a numerical clinical outcome.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for table transcription; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: Table 39 lists false-lumen patency and partial false-lumen thrombosis or occlusion as positive predictors of chronic-phase enlargement.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: False-lumen patency; Partial thrombosis/occlusion of a false lumen
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 偽腔開存、および偽腔の部分血栓化・部分閉塞。 Baseline imaging predictors; not post-treatment remodeling outcomes.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for table transcription; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: Table 39 lists an oval true lumen with round false lumen and a false lumen or entry on the lesser-curvature side as positive predictors of chronic-phase enlargement.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Oval true lumen/round false lumen; A false lumen or entry on the lesser curvature side
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 楕円形の真腔・円形の偽腔、および小弯側の偽腔またはエントリー。 Source-specific morphologic terminology is preserved.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for table transcription; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: Table 39 lists a single entry, false-lumen diameter at least 22 mm, and large entry at least 10 mm as positive predictors of chronic-phase enlargement.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: A single entry; False lumen diameter: ≥22 mm; large entry: ≥10 mm
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 単一エントリー、偽腔径22 mm以上、大きなエントリー10 mm以上。 These are baseline predictor thresholds, not clinical outcomes.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for table transcription; pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: Table 39 lists heart rate no more than 60/min, calcium-antagonist use, aortic diameter below 40 mm, round true lumen with oval false lumen, complete false-lumen thrombosis or occlusion, and numerous entries/re-entries as negative predictors of chronic-phase enlargement.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Heart rate: ≤60/min; Use of Ca antagonists; Aortic diameter: <40 mm; Round true lumen/oval false lumen; Complete thrombosis/occlusion of a false lumen; A large number of entries/re-entries
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 慢性期拡大の陰性予測因子として、心拍数60/分以下、Ca拮抗薬使用、大動脈径40 mm未満、円形真腔・楕円形偽腔、偽腔完全血栓化・閉塞、多数のエントリー・リエントリーが列挙されている。 Association in a guideline table does not prove a protective treatment effect.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for table transcription; validation strength pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The follow-up section identifies acute/subacute aortic diameter at least 40 mm, false-lumen diameter at least 22 mm, and partial false-lumen thrombosis as reported risk factors for chronic aortic events and calls for more careful imaging follow-up.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: As risk factors for aorta-associated events in the chronic phase, an aortic diameter of ≥40 mm in the acute/subacute phases, a false lumen diameter of ≥ 22 mm, and imaging findings such as partial thrombosis of a false lumen have been reported. In corresponding or similar patients, follow-up by imaging must be more carefully performed.
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.3 Uncomplicated Dissection, d. Medical Treatment, ii. Follow-up by Diagnostic Imaging
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 慢性期の大動脈関連イベントの危険因子として、急性期・亜急性期の大動脈径40 mm以上、偽腔径22 mm以上、偽腔部分血栓化などの画像所見が報告されており、該当・類似例では画像フォローをより慎重に行う必要がある。 This supports surveillance intensity, not automatic TEVAR benefit.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; prognostic and treatment implications pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: This guideline retains an IRAD-derived five-part complicated-dissection definition, while noting that SVS/STS classifies persistent pain, uncontrollable hypertension, and large or rapidly enlarging aorta as high-risk rather than complicated.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: However, in the ‘Reporting standard for Type B aortic dissection’ recently reported from the Society for Vascular Surgery (SVS)/Society of Thoracic Surgeons (STS), the above (1) and (2) criteria were classified as complicated dissection, and the (3) to (5) criteria as uncomplicated dissection. In addition, the latter was classified as ‘high-risk aortic dissection’ ... However, in most references quoted in this section, complicated dissection was evaluated in accordance with the IRAD classification; the (1) to (5) criteria were regarded as ‘complicated’ in this guideline.
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.2 Complicated Dissection, a. Definition
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: SVS/STS基準では破裂・臓器灌流障害を合併型、持続・反復する疼痛、制御不能高血圧、大径または急速拡大を高リスク非合併型とする一方、本ガイドラインは引用文献との整合性からIRADの5項目すべてを合併型として扱う。 This is a material cross-guideline classification difference.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; classification application pending specialist review

extracted_claims.claim_text: The guideline calls for future randomized evaluation using patient selection based on predicted enlargement and states that evidence for acute-phase preemptive TEVAR is limited.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Concerning the timing of preemptive TEVAR, there is an opinion that it should be performed in the acute phase (≤14 days after onset), but there is little evidence. ... In the future, a randomized controlled trial (RCT) should be conducted under patient selection based on the prediction of enlargement.
extracted_claims.section: Chapter VI, 3.1.1 Indications of Treatment: Overview
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Japanese translation — not original-source wording: 先制的TEVARを急性期（発症後14日以内）に行う意見はあるが、エビデンスは乏しく、今後は拡大予測に基づく患者選択を用いたRCTが必要である。
extracted_claims.confidence: High for English transcription; pending specialist review

guideline_recommendations.recommendation: Medical treatment is recommended for acute-phase patients
guideline_recommendations.population: Uncomplicated acute-phase Stanford type B aortic dissection
guideline_recommendations.japanese_translation_not_original_source: 急性期患者には内科的治療が推奨される。

guideline_recommendations.recommendation: Preemptive TEVAR should be considered in the subacute to early chronic phase for patients whose false lumen is expected to enlarge in the future: high-risk uncomplicated case
guideline_recommendations.population: High-risk uncomplicated Stanford type B dissection with predicted future false-lumen enlargement
guideline_recommendations.japanese_translation_not_original_source: 将来、偽腔拡大が予測される患者、すなわち高リスク非合併症例では、亜急性期から早期慢性期の先制的TEVARを考慮すべきである。

guideline_recommendations.recommendation: Preemptive TEVAR may be considered in the acute phase
guideline_recommendations.population: Uncomplicated acute-phase Stanford type B aortic dissection considered for preemptive TEVAR
guideline_recommendations.japanese_translation_not_original_source: 急性期の先制的TEVARを考慮してもよい。

guideline_recommendations.recommendation: TEVAR is recommended for acute/subacute phases patients
guideline_recommendations.population: Complicated acute/subacute Stanford type B aortic dissection
guideline_recommendations.japanese_translation_not_original_source: 急性期・亜急性期の合併症例にはTEVARが推奨される。

limitations: The authoritative publisher PDF is English. Every Japanese rendering in this review is explicitly labeled as a translation and is not original-source wording.

Table 39 combines demographic, physiologic, genetic, laboratory, medication, and imaging predictors. It does not establish that each predictor is independently validated or predicts benefit from TEVAR.

The guideline uses an IRAD-derived five-item complicated-dissection definition while explaining that SVS/STS reclassifies persistent pain, uncontrollable hypertension, and large or rapidly enlarging aorta as high-risk uncomplicated disease.
The selective subacute-to-early-chronic recommendation is Class IIa, Level B; the acute-phase option is Class IIb, Level B and the narrative explicitly describes limited evidence and controversy.
The guideline's early-chronic window extends through 1 year after onset, which differs from terminology used in the US and European guidelines.
The source warns against applying TEVAR to all false-lumen-patent uncomplicated TBAD and calls for selection based on predicted enlargement or aneurysm formation.
The measurements and thresholds in Table 39 are baseline predictors, not numerical clinical outcomes. No outcome records were created for them.
Post-TEVAR complications and post-treatment remodeling findings were not extracted as baseline selection criteria.
The source calls for future randomized evaluation using selection based on enlargement prediction; direct evidence that each risk feature identifies treatment benefit remains absent.
contradictions: Cross-guideline classification tension: persistent/recurrent pain, uncontrollable hypertension, and large or rapidly enlarging aorta are retained as complicated features under this guideline's IRAD-based framework but described as high-risk uncomplicated features under SVS/STS reporting standards.
Timing-strength distinction: subacute-to-early-chronic preemptive TEVAR is Class IIa/Level B, whereas acute-phase preemptive TEVAR is Class IIb/Level B and is described as controversial.

Compared with EACTS/STS, this guideline explicitly includes an early-chronic window through 1 year and prints a higher evidence level for selective preemptive TEVAR; these differences should not be harmonized without specialist interpretation.
reviewer_notes: Q03 extraction from the official English-language publisher PDF. Exact English wording is authoritative. Japanese translations are navigation aids only and are never represented as original quotations. All specialist decisions remain Pending; verified_by_reviewer and suitable_for_generated_answer remain false.
